docs.title: [App] Documentation
docs.text: Welcome to the [App] documentation. Use the navigation on the left to find what you need, or start with the links below.

docs.title: Where do you want to start?
docs.text:
• New to [App]? → Quick Start Guide
  • Setting up a room for the first time? → Room Setup
  • Something not working? → Troubleshooting
  • Looking for advanced configuration? → Advanced Features

docs.title: What you need
docs.text:
Before getting started, make sure you have:

  • A computer with a web browser
  • An XR device with the [App] app installed, connected to Wi-Fi

docs.title: Latest Release
docs.text: See the Release Notes for the latest updates.

(Add previous release notes here)

Staying Up to Date

We recommend bookmarking this page. Release notes are published here whenever a new version of [App] is released.

docs.title: Login Issues
docs.text: Can't log in to the Portal Make sure you're using the correct organizational email. If you see a message that your organization could not be found, contact your [App] administrator.

docs.title: Startup Issues
docs.text:
"Could not connect to [App] Services" message Ensure your device is connected to Wi-Fi.

Device can't recognize the QR code

  • Make sure the device camera is pointed directly at the QR code
  • Ensure the entire QR code is visible and not cut off
  • Try displaying the QR code on a larger screen, or print it at a larger size

docs.title: Anchor Issues
docs.text:
The Anchor is in the wrong position

Work through these checks in order:

  1. Verify the Anchor is locked — there should be no floating sphere visible and no ambient audio
  2. Make sure the device's cameras are not obscured. Walk around the room to improve spatial mapping — the Anchor may jump into place on its own
  3. If the Anchor is wrong on one device only, use the Anchor Menu on that device to reposition
  4. If the Anchor is wrong on many or all devices, the Anchor position may need to be reset — see Advanced Features
  5. If the issue persists, try switching to a Manual Anchor type — see Anchor Types

docs.title: Content Issues
docs.text:
An object isn't loading

  • Make sure [App] is open on your device and you're in the correct room
  • If content isn't loading for any user, verify the Anchor Code was entered correctly
  • Large models may take time to load depending on Wi-Fi speed and device capabilities — if you see a loading indicator, wait
  • If no loading indicator appears after a while, close the [App] app completely, reopen it, and rejoin your room

Can't move an object

  • Verify that the content is supposed to be movable — not all objects can be moved
  • Ensure all device permissions have been accepted
  • Try quitting and restarting the [App] app
  • If the object cannot be moved by multiple users and is disrupting the session, your administrator can use Asset Sender to remove all objects from the room

docs.title: General Device Issues
docs.text: If a problem is isolated to a single XR device, close the app completely and rejoin. If that doesn't help, fully shut down the device (press and hold the power button), wait 15–30 seconds, then power it back on.

docs.title: Advanced Features
docs.text: This section covers features intended for room administrators and power users.

docs.title: Managing Anchors
docs.text:
To manage Anchors for a room:

  1. From the Portal, select Rooms → Manage Rooms
  2. Select the room you want to modify
  3. Click Manage Anchors from the operations menu

docs.title: Adding Additional Anchors
docs.text:
You can create multiple Anchors in a single room for more complex setups.

  1. In the Manage Anchors dialog, click Add Anchor
  2. Enter an Anchor name
  3. Select an Anchor type (see below)
  4. Confirm — then follow the standard steps to place and lock the new Anchor in XR

docs.title: Anchor Types
docs.text: Type When to use Automatic Default. Use in most cases. The first Automatic Anchor in a room is designated "Center." Subsequent Automatic Anchors are shared between devices based on the Center Anchor's position. Manual Use if devices are struggling with automatic alignment, or for temporary setups where the Anchor position may change frequently. Must be placed individually per device and is not synced between users. Compound Advanced use only. Creates an Anchor centered between two existing Anchors.

docs.title: Notes on Automatic Anchors
docs.text:
• Non-center Automatic Anchors can be moved by one user when unlocked, and changes sync to all devices in the room
  • Non-center Automatic Anchor positions can also be adjusted numerically (in meters) from the Portal

docs.title: Updating an Individual Anchor
docs.text:
Select an Anchor from the list in the Manage Anchors dialog, then choose from the toolbar:

  • Edit — update Anchor name or add it to a Local Anchor Group
  • Reset — unlocks the Anchor for all users so it can be repositioned
    • Not available for the Center Anchor
    • All users in the room will need to reposition and re-lock after a reset
  • Delete — removes the Anchor
    • All rooms must have at least one Anchor

docs.title: Refine Anchors
docs.text:
Use this when an Anchor is out of place on one device only (similar to Reposition in the Anchor Menu, but initiated from the Portal).

  1. In XR, locate the misaligned Anchor and note its 4-letter Anchor Code
  2. In the Portal, enter that code where prompted in the Manage Anchors dialog
  3. Click Refine — [App] will unlock the Anchor so it can be repositioned on that device

docs.title: Clear Room Map
docs.text:
This resets the spatial map that the Center Anchor's position is based on. Use sparingly — the Center Anchor is intended to be permanent.

Use only if:

  • All devices are having trouble locating the Center Anchor, or
  • The room's physical layout has significantly changed since initial setup

What happens after clearing:

  • The next device to join the room will re-scan and re-place the Center Anchor for everyone
  • Devices that previously bypassed alignment and placed manually may retain their old Anchor positions — adjust these individually via the Anchor Menu

Warning

Clearing the room map affects all users. Coordinate with your team before doing this during an active session.

docs.title: The Anchor Menu
docs.text: While in [App], tapping on the Anchor opens a menu with three options. This menu is visible only to you — other users in the room are not affected.

docs.title: Leave Room
docs.text:
Use this to switch rooms without restarting the app.

  • Grab the red "Leave Room" cube
  • Enter or scan a new room code when prompted

This is useful if you accidentally joined the wrong room.

docs.title: Close Menu
docs.text: Closes the Anchor menu without taking any action. Tapping the Anchor again also closes the menu.

docs.title: Reposition Center for Me
docs.text:
Use this if your Anchor is misaligned from the physical floor marker in your room. This adjusts the Anchor for your device only — other users are not affected.

  1. Grab the blue "Reposition Center for Me" cube — this unlocks the floor Anchor for your device
  2. Follow the Manual Placement steps to move and re-lock the Anchor

docs.title: Sending Content to XR
docs.text: Once your room is set up and your Anchor is placed, you're ready to send content to your XR devices.

docs.title: Find your Anchor Code
docs.text:
Your Anchor displays a 4-letter code next to it in XR. You'll need this to connect apps to your device.

  • Each device sees a different code, but all codes work
  • Enter this code when prompted by any [App] application

docs.title: Open an App from the Portal
docs.text:
[App] includes pre-built applications you can control directly from your browser.

  1. From the Portal homepage, select Apps
  2. Click any application to open its controller in a new tab
  3. Enter your 4-letter Anchor Code in the field provided
    • On most apps, this is a box in the top-right corner labeled "None" until a code is entered
  4. The app is now connected — content you send from the browser will appear on your XR device

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• If content doesn't appear, double-check that your Anchor Code was entered correctly
  • Large models may take time to load depending on your Wi-Fi speed and device
  • If you see a loading indicator, wait — if nothing happens after a while, close and reopen the [App] app and rejoin your room
  • Each user in a room can have a different Anchor Code displayed — any of them will work

docs.title: Quick Start Guide
docs.text: This guide walks a room administrator through setting up [App] for the first time. One person per room completes this setup.

docs.title: Step 1: Sign In to the [App] Portal
docs.text:
On your computer, go to the [App] Portal.

  1. Click Sign In in the top right corner
  2. Choose your sign-in method:
    • Use your organizational email address
    • If your organization uses Google SSO, select Continue with Google
  3. After signing in, you'll see the Portal homepage with three options: Rooms, Meetings, and Apps

docs.title: Step 2: Create a Room
docs.text:
1. Select Rooms → Create Room
  2. Enter your room information:
    • Name (required): Match this to the physical location, e.g. "Conference Room B" or "Anna's Home Office"
    • Description (optional)
  3. Click Next

docs.title: Create your Anchor
docs.text:
Anchors help XR devices align content in your physical space.

  1. Click Add Anchor
  2. Leave the Name field blank — [App] will auto-name it "Center"
  3. Set Type to Automatic
  4. Leave Is Center checked
  5. Leave Anchor Groups blank
  6. Click Add Anchor in the bottom right to confirm

docs.title: Save your Room
docs.text:
1. Confirm you see "Anchor Name: Center" and "Type: Automatic" in the summary
  2. Click Next, then Finish
  3. A dialog will appear with your Room Name, a 6-digit room code, and a QR code

Save your Room Code

You'll need the QR code or 6-digit code to connect XR devices to this room. We recommend printing the QR code and posting it in the room for easy access.

docs.title: Step 3: Connect your XR Device
docs.text:
1. Start up your XR headset and make sure the cameras are not obscured
  2. Walk around the room for 10–15 seconds so the device can orient itself to the space
  3. Launch the [App] app from your device's home menu
  4. Accept any permission prompts (camera, microphone)
  5. Connect to your Room using one of these methods:
    • Scan QR Code — point the device camera at the QR code from Step 2 (HoloLens, Mobile)
    • Enter Room Code — use the number pad in XR to type the 6-digit code (all other devices)

docs.title: Step 4: Position your Anchor
docs.text:
The first device to join a new room sets the Anchor position for everyone. Only one device needs to complete this — additional devices should skip to Joining from Additional Devices.

See the full Room Setup guide for detailed Anchor placement instructions.

docs.title: You're Ready!
docs.text: Once your Anchor is placed, you can start sending content to XR. See Sending Content to XR to get started.

docs.title: Room Setup
docs.text: This page covers Anchor placement in detail, including first-time setup, joining from additional devices, and rejoining an existing room.

docs.title: Finding an Existing Room
docs.text:
If a room has already been set up by someone else in your organization:

  1. From the Portal homepage, select Rooms → Manage Rooms

  2. Find and select the room you want to join from the list

    Warning

    This list contains all rooms in your organization. Do not delete or edit rooms that belong to other users.

  3. Click Show QR Code from the operations menu to retrieve the QR code and 6-digit code for that room

docs.title: First-Time Anchor Placement
docs.text: The first device to join a new room is responsible for placing the Anchor for everyone. Subsequent devices skip to Joining from Additional Devices.

docs.title: Scan the Room
docs.text:
• Look around the room — blue dots will appear on nearby surfaces
  • Walk around until blue dots cover the walls, floor, and furniture
  • Grab the green "Yes" cube when the scan is complete

No blue dots appearing?

Your device may not support room scanning. Grab the red "No, Place Center Manually" cube instead — this lets you place the Anchor for your device only. The next device to join will place the Anchor for everyone.

docs.title: Place the Anchor
docs.text:
Figure 1 — Unlocked Anchor
  1. Locate the unlocked Anchor — a floating sphere above a circular marker on the floor
  2. Grab the sphere and drag it to your desired position in the room
  3. Grab the grey "Lock" cube to confirm placement
  4. When locked, only the circular floor Anchor remains (with a "Center" label and a 4-letter code)

docs.title: Mark the Anchor Location
docs.text:
Place a physical marker — a sticker, piece of tape, or printed label — on the floor directly under the holographic Anchor. Point the arrow on your marker in the same direction as the holographic arrow.

This marker helps future users and additional devices confirm correct alignment.

docs.title: Joining from Additional Devices
docs.text: After the Anchor is placed by the first device, other devices follow this process.

docs.title: Automatic Alignment
docs.text:
Some devices only need to complete this once, the first time they join a room:

  • HoloLens
  • Meta Quest
  • Apple Vision Pro

Other devices must complete this every session, or place manually:

  • XReal
  • Mobile devices

Steps:

  1. Scan the room — look around until blue dots cover all surfaces
    • If no blue dots appear, select the red "Place Manually" cube and follow Manual Placement below
  2. Grab the green "Yes" cube when complete
  3. Set floor level — confirm the blue target is on the floor, or drag the sphere to adjust
  4. Wait for [App] to align the room automatically
  5. Confirm Anchor placement — look for blue particles to locate the Anchor
    • If correct: grab the green "Yes" cube
    • If incorrect: return to scan, or use manual placement
  6. Once confirmed, only the circular floor Anchor with its name and 4-letter code will remain

docs.title: Manual Placement
docs.text:
Use this if automatic alignment fails or your device cannot scan the room.

  1. Locate the unlocked Anchor (floating sphere above the circular floor marker)
  2. Grab the sphere and drag it to align with the physical floor marker in your room
  3. Make sure the holographic arrow matches the direction of your floor marker
  4. Grab the grey "Lock" cube to confirm

docs.title: Rejoining a Room
docs.text:
Some headsets (HoloLens, Meta Quest, Apple Vision Pro) remember Anchor placement between sessions. When you rejoin, the locked Anchor should appear automatically in its last known position.

If the Anchor position looks incorrect, use the Anchor Menu to reposition it. See The Anchor Menu for details.
